title: How does your Eva fandom compare
display_title: How does your Eva fandom compare
wikitext:
[]: How does your Eva fandom compare?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 29, 2004 11:47 pm How does your interest in Eva compare to that of other anime? Eva is a show that can get people hooked pretty strongly. For me, there's not much other anime out there worth getting caught up in. I really liked .hack//SIGN, FLCL, His and Her Circumstances, and Trigun. And I absolutely loved Millenium Actress and Grave of the Fireflies. Aside from anime, I'm a big Alias fan. But in general, I find it hard to get caught up in an anime the way I have with Eva, I got really into Haibane Renmei, and devoured the episodes, but once I was done with watching it, my thoughts didn't remain with it, I moved on to the next thing. And nothing since Eva has been able to capture my soul in that same way.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Wed Jun 30, 2004 12:12 am In my case, i'm not even close to an otaku. Mi favorite anime is Evangelion, and the other that i like are those that are shown in TV in my country (dragon ball Z, samurai X, ranma1/2, etc). Before i watched Eva, i was intrigued by it, because everybody told me that was about humanity fighting the angels, and i said "wow!, thats something more dense and complex", and years later Evangelion was on the TV and i was fascinated.

[]:             Quote Post by asher_fuller » Wed Jun 30, 2004 12:15 am I feel the same way (about being hooked on Eva) . I've been a huge anime fan for about 7 years now (and I'm only 15), and I haven't found many animes that has have me as hooked as Evangelion. I've seen LOTS of animes and read tons of mangas, but only a few of them have come close and possibly surpassed Eva (and I'm still debating if anything is better). I'm not sure what it is about Evangelion, but it is very in-depth and has made me really think hard about its message. Every character is well designed, and I feel I can relate to/sympathise with all of them. As an artist, this series has changed the way I look at anime, not only visually, but also in the direction and meaning of the show. It really shows what people can create using a pen, paper, and creative mind. Evangelion will definitley influence all of my artwork and stories in the future, and I can always look to it for inspiration.

[]:             Quote Post by TheMessiah » Wed Jun 30, 2004 12:18 am eva got me into seriously thinking about theology for a major in college, hows them apples for you

[]:             Quote Post by asher_fuller » Wed Jun 30, 2004 12:19 am i love theology

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Wed Jun 30, 2004 12:23 am There are very few pieces of entertainemnt that will hook people like Eva does. With most anime, you watch it, enjoy it, and then your'e done. But with Eva there is so much to think about and discuss that you really are not done with it after watching it.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Wed Jun 30, 2004 1:57 am I havent really been that captivated by a series since I first saw Evangelion. It was the first animes that made me go; "Oh holy hell, wtf?! Need more.. NOW!" Everything else is good, but the only thing that came close was S.E. Lain... and probably something that I'm forgetting, but I'm allowed to forget since I've been a wake for 30hrs. Neon Genesis is, by far, my favorite after all these years. I tend to enjoy series that make me think, rather then just mindlessly laugh at stupid overdone jokes. Evangelion did that for me. Probably not a 'super eva otaku phreak' but I've got a pretty firm grasp on most of the series/movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Quiddity » Wed Jun 30, 2004 6:29 am I'm much more of a casual fan of Eva than probably most other members of this forum. Its in my top 10 favorite anime, EOE is my favorite anime movie, and I love discussing it on messageboards like this, but I'm not gonna go and say its changed my outlook on life, or even anime for that matter. Its a nice series I enjoyed watching and enjoy rewatching, but there's better stuff out there like Fooly Cooly and Cowboy Bebop.

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Wed Jun 30, 2004 7:31 am Eva is by far my favorite anime. But I'm interested in Trigun, Rah-Xephon, Furi Kuri, BoogiePop Phantom, and Ranma 1/2. Oh, :oops: ,of-course-there's-also-digimon :oops: ...

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Wed Jun 30, 2004 8:39 am Hmm, probably the only series I've probably been as wrapped up with is Cowboy Bebop. Bebop didn't really touch on the philosophical stuff as much but it had great characters, which is why I love Eva, too. But, in the end, Eva is my most favorite anime ever.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Wed Jun 30, 2004 10:02 am I'll admit it......... I'm a huge Gundam fan........ I think I can still tell you the dry wieght of an MS-06J Zaku Ground Type cold if I thought about it.....

[]:             Quote Post by Vulkurt » Wed Jun 30, 2004 10:22 am It's odd, because I have a pretty huge anime collection. Recently, my cousin visited me and told me to pop in some anime that had "huge fighting mechs." I was at a loss, because all I could come up with was Evangelion and Voices of a Distant Star , neither of which really fit the "huge fighting mechs" genre very well. I tend to go for either things that warp my fragile mind (Evangelion fits well there) or comedy (er, doesn't fit there as well). I can go a year without watching Evangelion, then sit down and watch the whole thing from start to finish in a day. So, as far as my Evangelion fandom outshining my the rest of my collection, it really doesn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Jun 30, 2004 12:06 pm Besides Evangelion, the only other animes I've seen that even come close are RahXephon and the .hack project. But even after two years I'm still just as obsessed with eva as when I first watched it. I must have seen it at least fifty times and it just keeps getting better.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Jun 30, 2004 2:03 pm I'm a fan of all of Yoshitoshi ABe's work, and FLCL also kept me captivated a lot longer than 6 episodes normally would. I'm just as big a fan of Evangelion as I am FLCL, and Haibane Renmei.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Wed Jun 30, 2004 3:10 pm When I started to get into anime (somewhere in 3rd or 4th grade-- I'm in 10th now), it was Sailor Moon. All day, every day. While I still respect the show today as being much more than just a crappily dubbed kid's show (the original version had SO much more depth and meaning, making it still one of my favorite series), when I first got into it, that's pretty much all I took it for-- a show with pretty girls and cool magical powers. This is pretty much all I can compare to Eva in terms of how much I REALLY liked an anime series. The only difference is that with Eva, it's pretty hard to just take everything at face value. It's fun to look into the psychological aspects of the show (among other aspects) and come out of it saying, "Oh yeah, I see where they're coming from. I think I can relate to this event/character because... " While I did that later on with Sailor Moon as well, there's just something about Eva that never ceases to interest me. Probably because you always find new information and things that you otherwise might have missed. Plus, it's the only series I spent $130 on buying the entire DVD box set, so that says something, too. (I never spend more than $50 dollars on any kind of anime merchandise, and even if I do, I think about it for at least a week. I bought the stupid thing within 2 days.) Eva is also the only really serious anime I watch on a regular basis. I like watching comedies and occasionally action series' better than watching shows that provide no comic relief and/or make you depressed (and Goddamn, did Eva put me in a slump for a good week and a half). Excel Saga, Steel Angel Kurumi, Azumanga Daioh, Dirty Pair, Cowboy Bebop... are the ones I enjoy watching along with Eva. And yes, I admit I'm a magical girl fan. (And I like Digimon, too.)

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Jun 30, 2004 3:34 pm For me, EVA is the only series that I own in entirety and the one I like the best (although since a lot of people keep talking about it I am starting to get into Cowboy Bebop). I like a lot of the ones mentioned above, but I tend just to rent them instead of buying them. Also there is nothing wrong with liking digimon.

[]:             Quote Post by Slade » Wed Jun 30, 2004 4:30 pm Im more of a casual fan as well, I dont need to watch the series over and over again, otherwise Id be bored with it. Ive also almost always been hooked on Bebop, I can watch that show over and over and not get bored with it. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Alien Guy » Wed Jun 30, 2004 9:46 pm For Me Eva is the best Anime series. I enjoy others, like Trigun and Cowboy bebop, but eva is the only series that invaded my mind. The only other thing that i'm a huge fan of like that of Eva is Horror movies.

[]: eva             Quote Post by the-artist-known-as-chris » Sun Jul 11, 2004 10:12 pm I love anime in genral, but I just adore Eva, it's one of the best works of fiction ever created. Im constanly collecting eva stuff too, I have figures of Misato, Asuka, Rei, Shinji, pen pen, Ritsko and Maya, aswell as speical Asuka and Rei figures with interchangible heads and limbs, highly detailed. Wallscrolls, posters, a mouse pad, various unit 1 figures and other evangelions, many DVDs, a Sacheial figure, a eva series figure...bah, I cant keep track anymore...oh yeah I also have the first 8 volumes of manga.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Jul 11, 2004 10:14 pm I have now over 100 wallpapers , yea , of eva

Eva is a show that can get people hooked pretty strongly. For me, there's not much other anime out there worth getting caught up in. I really liked .hack//SIGN, FLCL, His and Her Circumstances, and Trigun. And I absolutely loved Millenium Actress and Grave of the Firefli
